JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
MINI-MINUTES FOR AUG-12-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS DARREN MATTE, JOHN CHRISTIANSEN, AND, MICHELLE WOHL MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. ARBITRATION CLAUSE BINDS JOHN KELLEY. (SEE REASONING IN KELLER CONST. CO., INC. VS. KASHANI (1990) 220 CAL.APP.3D 222.) ARBITRATION CLAUSE BINDS KELLEY'S TAVERN LLC WHICH ADOPTED AND RATIFIED THE PRE-INCORPORATION AGREEMENTS EXECUTED ON ITS BEHALF. THE LOOSE CLAIM DOES NOT EXEMPT THIS CONTROVERSY FROM ARBITRATION. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT WAIVED THEIR RIGHT TO ARBITRATE. ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Apr-05-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Brian V. Donnelly, Christopher J. Keller and Cornerstone Law Group Motion to be relieved as counsel for DEFENDANT KELLEY'S TAVERN. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. No opposition filed. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/PHA) Judge: Paul H. Alvarado; Court Reporter: Nichole Rodich, CSR #11604

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-09-2009 Continued To Mar-19-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Aug-19-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS DARREN MATTE, JOHN CHRISTIANSEN, and, MICHELLE WOHL Motion to Compel Arbitration. Appearances: A. Peter Rausch, Jr. Law Offices of A. Peter Rausch, Jr., appearing for Plaintiffs and moving party; Christopher Keller, Esq. of Cornerstone Law Group (415) 357-2086, appearing for Defendant Kelley's Tavern. The tentative ruling is not being contested. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to compel arbitration is granted. Arbitration clause binds John Kelley. (See reasoning in Keller Const. Co., Inc. vs. Kashani (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 222.) Arbitration clause binds Kelley's Tavern LLC which adopted and ratified the pre-incorporation agreements executed on its behalf. The Loose claim does not exempt this controversy from arbitration. Plaintiffs have not waived their right to arbitrate. Order Compelling Arbitration signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
